commit.message:
v0.2.47: midnight streak decay scheduler

- Add decay_streaks() in gamification.py: decrements streak by 1 per missed day
- Tracks last_streak_decay_date in config table for catch-up after restarts
- Call decay_streaks() at day rollover in _scheduler_loop (main.py)
- Revert update_streak to simple +1 on first daily completion (no lazy decrement)
- Update tests: new TestDecayStreaks class with 4 tests for decay logic
- Bump to v0.2.47

fix: force staging for known-large TLDs + promote silent exceptions to WARNING

- Add _ALWAYS_STAGE_TLDS frozenset: com, net, org, top always use staging
  regardless of pg_class.reltuples availability (transient failures caused
  .com to fall back to direct upsert → GIN index bottleneck → infinite loop)
- Promote _get_tld_domain_count exception from logger.debug to logger.warning
  so transient DB failures are visible in production logs
- Add explicit logger.info in _should_use_staging to log staging decision
  (staging forced / count-based / fallback-warning) for every TLD
- Promote _batch_size_for_tld exception to logger.warning

commit.message:
Refactor tournament architecture and expand deterministic test stack (#6)

* Refactor tournament architecture and expand deterministic test stack

* Extract game bot core helpers and extend deterministic test coverage

* Add shared game session handoff between tournament and main view

* Refactor game rendering module

* Refactor game controls module

* Refactor game selection module

* Refactor game match UI module

* Extract game settings module and add structure tuning

* Extract battle test module and add cheapest bot

* Extract debug API, redesign settings persistence for localStorage on refresh with lowest defaults and reset button

- Extract window.legionDebug into game-debug-api.js (~198 lines)
  - Tunnel simulation/constants/state/HUD update functions via dependency injection
  - Support all debug tuning methods (spawn, peon, structure, economy, decision log)
  - Factory export: createGameDebugApi

- Redesign settings persistence behavior
  - Add LOWEST_SETTINGS constant (~30 numeric value defaults)
  - getSavedSettingsOrLowest(): load from localStorage, fallback to lowest values if empty
  - resetToDefaultSettings(): clear storage and restore original defaults (version-specific)
  - ensureVersionDefaultsSaved(): preserve defaults under separate key for reset button
  - Modified init() sequence: load from storage on every refresh, use lowest if missing

- Add 'Reset to Defaults' button to UI
  - New button#resetDefaultsBtn in devSection
  - Wired in game-controls.js: cleared storage, reloaded defaults, re-synced all controls
  - Works alongside (not replacing) in-session persistence

- Wire debug API into game.js with safe fallback (|| {}) if module fails to load

All node tests pass (88 total tests).
game.js reduced to 407 lines (down from 739 at session start).

* Add comprehensive refactor summary for merge review

Final documentation of 11-commit refactor:
- 13 modularized game modules (down from 1 monolith)
- 6 test suites with 94 deterministic tests
- 81% reduction in game.js (2196 → 407 lines)
- Complete module catalog and architecture design
- Pre-merge checklist and impact analysis

* Add agent reference documentation for architecture (was untracked)

* Fix reset defaults wiring and clarify agent architecture notes

- Inject resetToDefaultSettings into createGameControls
- Fix setupResetDefaultsButton to avoid undefined references
- Export setupResetDefaultsButton from controls API
- Keep game.js wiring by passing reset handler from settings manager
- Remove inaccurate merge status line from agent architecture note
- Clarify RNG statement to deterministic simulation core scope

* Apply PR review feedback: localStorage safety, XSS fix, CI dedup, indentation, settings order

commit.message:
v0.2.46: decremental streak loss instead of hard reset

Each missed day reduces streak by 1 (min 0). Formula on completion:
  new_streak = max(0, current_streak - missed_days) + 1
where missed_days = days_since_last_completion - 1.

Updated test: test_streak_decrements_after_gap + test_streak_bottoms_at_zero

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

to English-trained Gemma models on non-English devices.

FunctionGemmaRouter.

commit.message:
fix(ast_lowering): use full param span for enum/struct destructuring patterns

When a function parameter is an enum/struct destructuring pattern such as
`Page { title, alerts, posts }`, `get_param_names` correctly lowercased the
struct name to use as the synthesised parameter name (e.g. `page`), but then
only looked for `PatKind::Identifier` spans when computing `source_span`.
Enum patterns fell through to `unwrap_or_default()`, yielding `Span::default()`
(position 0,0), so inlay hints for these parameters were placed at the very
beginning of the file.

Fix by also handling `PatKind::Enum` in the source-span lookup, using the
full `FunctionParameter.span` (which covers the entire pattern including the
closing `}`).  This places the type hint right after the closing brace, which
is the natural position for an annotation following a destructured parameter.

Add a regression test in `tlang_analysis` that verifies no hint from a struct
pattern parameter lands at position (0, 0).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: test-cov mise task silently drops CLI args; profile test wrong on 3.14 (#331)

* fix: add usage field to test-cov task and fix testing profile test for Python 3.14

The test-cov mise task silently dropped -m markers because it had no
usage field defined. CI marker expressions like 'not dev_only' were
never applied, causing dev_only/docker/performance tests to run and
fail in CI.

Also fix test_testing_profile_uses_ultralight_constants to account for
HAS_FASTEMBED=False on Python 3.14, where sparse embedding uses
ibm-granite/granite-embedding-30m-sparse instead of qdrant/bm25.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/knitli/codeweaver/sessions/4b9a7c93-cd45-4002-95fa-66e63b8b6981

* fix: eliminate eval in test-cov, parametrize profile test over HAS_FASTEMBED

Address review feedback:
- Replace eval+string-building with POSIX-safe `set --` argument list
  so each arg is passed as its own token (no shell injection risk)
- Parametrize test_testing_profile_uses_ultralight_constants over
  HAS_FASTEMBED=True/False so both backend paths are validated
  deterministically in every test run
- Fix: ST branch reranking assertion now correctly expects
  ULTRALIGHT_ST_RERANKING_MODEL (was asserting fastembed constant)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/knitli/codeweaver/sessions/435f7a0d-d419-41c7-a426-7b93ef59a126

style: Text im Entnahme-Löschdialog zentrieren

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Phase 1 Rust workspace — 5 crates, MCP server, ProcessSandbox

Implements the complete Phase 1 backend:

Crates:
- sandcastle-runtime: SandboxRuntime trait, types (SandboxId, ExecRequest,
  ExecResult, SandboxConfig, ResourceLimits), error types with MCP error codes
- sandcastle-executor: In-sandbox binary, reads JSON from stdin, spawns
  language runtime (python3/node/bash), captures output with timeout/OOM
- sandcastle-manager: Session registry, file validation (allowed_input_dirs,
  path traversal), reaper for expired sessions, oneshot + session execution
- sandcastle-process: ProcessSandbox using libcontainer (youki), OCI spec
  generation via oci-spec crate, namespace isolation (pid/mount/ipc/uts/net),
  pipe-based executor communication, workspace bind-mounts
- sandcastle-server: MCP server using rmcp crate, 6 tools (execute_code,
  create_sandbox, execute_in_session, upload_file, download_file,
  destroy_sandbox), stdio transport, reaper background task

Tests:
- 15 unit tests (9 runtime types, 5 manager with mock runtime, 1 OCI spec)
- Integration test script: executor direct test, timeout handling, MCP server
  tool listing

Tooling:
- scripts/build-rootfs.sh: Docker export rootfs builder for python/bash/node

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 14: Shimmer Animation (#33)

## Summary

**Phase 14: Shimmer Animation**
**Goal:** Users see a smooth animated skeleton shimmer while translation
is in progress, with no jarring layout shifts
**Status:** Verified ✓ (3/3 must-haves passed)

Adds a polished left-to-right gradient sweep shimmer animation to the
popup loading state. The shimmer plays from translation start until the
result appears, using `.plusLighter` blend mode that adapts to both
light and dark system appearance. Implemented as a zero-layout-impact
`.overlay` ViewModifier to avoid interfering with the popup's
GeometryReader height measurement. Fully respects the Reduce Motion
accessibility setting.

## Changes

### Plan 14-01: ShimmerModifier ViewModifier + Unit Tests
Left-to-right gradient sweep ViewModifier using `.plusLighter` blend
mode with Reduce Motion accessibility fallback.

**Key files created:**
- `Transy/Popup/ShimmerModifier.swift` — ViewModifier with animated
gradient sweep, `.clipped()`, `.allowsHitTesting(false)`
- `TransyTests/ShimmerModifierTests.swift` — 3 structural tests

### Plan 14-02: Wire Shimmer into LoadingPopupText
Connected `ShimmerModifier` to the actual loading UI via `.shimmer()` on
`PopupText` inside `LoadingPopupText.body`.

**Key files modified:**
- `Transy/Popup/PopupView.swift` — Added `.shimmer()` call on
loading-state text

## Requirements Addressed

| ID | Requirement | Status |
|----|------------|--------|
| SHM-01 | Shimmer animation plays on popup text during translation
loading | ✓ |
| SHM-02 | Zero layout impact — no resize notification storm | ✓ |
| SHM-03 | Reduce Motion disables animation — static muted text fallback
| ✓ |

## Verification

- [x] Automated verification: PASSED (3/3 must-haves)
- [x] Human visual verification: gradient sweep, no layout shift, Reduce
Motion compliance
- [x] Unit tests: all pass (`make test`)

## Key Decisions

- `.overlay` architecture (not ZStack) to prevent GeometryReader height
measurement interference
- `.plusLighter` blend mode for light/dark adaptive shimmer appearance
- 1.5s linear `repeatForever(autoreverses: false)` sweep speed
- `.onAppear` for animation start (not `.task`) per pitfall analysis

Closes #28

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feat(blueprint): Add read-before-write check

Before calling guesty.set_custom_field, read the current
value via guesty.get_custom_field_values and compare it to
the slot_code. Skip the write when the values already match.
This avoids unnecessary API calls on every periodic sync
and reduces Guesty API usage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

the buttons are disabled immediately (preventing double-press) and the card
stays in the DOM. Removal is now driven entirely by a backend SSE delete event
(animated) or a re-render on SSE action event. Buttons are only re-enabled
if the POST itself fails.
